notes: Streaming-partial-writes for antigravity — asserts the no-flicker invariant ready→working→ready with the band staying solidly working across the streaming window and no premature ready before the terminal turn-end signal. Like gemini-cli (single-write-per-turn) and UNLIKE claudecode (per-chunk intermediate lines): agy persists each step ONLY on completion (every transcript line is status:DONE) and writes NO intermediate no-tool PLANNER_RESPONSE prose chunk — every turn has exactly ONE no-tool PLANNER_RESPONSE, the terminal sentinel, with EMPTY content. The parser emits turn_done only on a no-tool PLANNER_RESPONSE, so there is no intermediate record to flicker on; the daemon emits exactly one working→ready, at turn end. Single-birth: session_id from the <conv-id> dir on file appearance, so session_birth anchors to start. PROVISIONAL on emission: record must live-confirm zero working→ready across the streaming window on a genuinely long PROSE-only turn. Tokens/cost not in the JSONL — no cost/token assertion. OBSERVABILITY CORRECTION (live recording 2026-06-20, 5a7754b3): agy writes the ENTIRE turn atomically at completion (one flush, status:DONE only — no mid-turn lines), so the daemon synthesizes working then ready ~1.7ms apart from that single flush. There is NO observable streaming window to hold for ≥1500ms (unlike gemini-cli, which streams its transcript incrementally and sustains ~20s of working). The duration_at_least_ms floor is therefore dropped — it asserts a streaming behavior atomic-write agy structurally cannot exhibit. The no-flicker invariant — exactly ONE working→ready, no intermediate ready, no waiting — DID hold and is what this cell verifies. The single terminal no-tool PLANNER_RESPONSE carried the prose (645 bytes) rather than empty content, but the parser still emits exactly one turn_done. OBSERVABILITY CORRECTION (live recording 2026-06-20, conv 5a7754b3): agy writes the ENTIRE turn atomically on completion (one flush, status:DONE only — no mid-turn lines), so the daemon synthesizes working then ready ~1.7ms apart from that single flush. There is NO observable streaming window for an atomic-write agent (unlike gemini-cli, which streams its transcript incrementally and sustains ~20s of working). The streaming_window phase (and its duration_at_least_ms:1500 hold) is therefore DROPPED — it asserts a streaming behavior agy structurally cannot exhibit. What this cell now verifies is the no-flicker invariant that DID hold: ready→working→ready with exactly ONE working→ready, no intermediate ready, no waiting. The single terminal no-tool PLANNER_RESPONSE carried the prose (645 bytes) rather than empty content; the parser still emits exactly one turn_done.

text: Session appears in ready shortly after the transcript file lands
Multi-sentence prose prompt drives ready → working

agy writes the whole turn atomically on completion (one flush, status:DONE), so the daemon synthesizes working then the single terminal no-tool PLANNER_RESPONSE → working → ready exactly once, with NO intermediate ready and NO waiting. This sole, clean working→ready is the no-flicker proof for an atomic-write agent (no observable streaming window exists, unlike incrementally-streaming agents).
